cards.back: A mathematical model for computation that consists of a tape divided into cells, each of which can hold a symbol from a finite alphabet
A decision problem in the theory of computation that asks whether a given Turing Machine will halt on a given input
A type of grammar used to describe the syntax of programming languages, where the production rules are finite and context-dependent
A pattern used to match strings in a specific way, often used in text processing and string manipulation
A language for which there exists an algorithm that can determine whether a given string belongs to the language
